title: daphne indica subsp. novae-caledoniae
intro: Daphne indica subsp. novae-caledoniae, also known as the New Caledonian laurel, is a subspecies of Daphne indica, a flowering plant native to the Pacific region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are beneficial in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Daphne indica subsp. novae-caledoniae include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting cardiovascular health, among others. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
